Planning Your 3-Day NYC Itinerary.question: What's the best order to see NYC landmarks in 3 days solo?
Planning Your 3-Day NYC Itinerary.answer: Day 1 in Midtown (Times Square, Top of the Rock, Central Park) gets the biggest sights done when you have the most energy. Day 2 for Downtown and Brooklyn — the Statue of Liberty ferry takes a half-day, then DUMBO in the afternoon. Day 3 for museums at your own pace — the Met or MoMA move faster solo since you don't have to wait for anyone. Book the Statue of Liberty ferry in advance regardless of which day you go.

Planning Your 3-Day NYC Itinerary.question: How much walking does a 3-day NYC solo itinerary actually involve?
Planning Your 3-Day NYC Itinerary.answer: Expect 5–8 miles per day on foot. Midtown is walkable between most attractions; getting between Manhattan and Brooklyn requires the subway (30–40 minutes each way). Wear shoes you can walk in for hours — sneakers, not sandals. A single OMNY tap with any contactless card handles all subway and bus rides at $2.90 each.

Planning Your 3-Day NYC Itinerary.question: Should I pre-book NYC attractions or can I show up and pay at the door?
Planning Your 3-Day NYC Itinerary.answer: Pre-book the Statue of Liberty and Ellis Island ferry — timed-entry slots fill weeks ahead and it gets crowded by 10am. Top of the Rock and One World Observatory are worth booking online for 15–20% off. The Met, MoMA, and Central Park need no booking. Broadway rush tickets and TKTS day-of discounts (Times Square booth) are worth checking for shows.

Solo Budget & Logistics.question: What's a realistic daily budget for a solo NYC itinerary?
Solo Budget & Logistics.answer: Budget $150–$220/day for a comfortable mid-range solo trip. Hotel: $150–$200/night (cheaper in Midtown East or the Financial District than Midtown West). Subway: $10–$15/day. Meals: $12–$18 for lunch (pizza, bagel, ramen), $30–$50 for dinner. One paid attraction per day runs $25–$35. Broadway tickets are $60–$120 depending on show and seat.

Solo Budget & Logistics.question: Where should a solo traveler base themselves for a 3-day NYC trip?
Solo Budget & Logistics.answer: Midtown East (Murray Hill or Kips Bay) gives the best balance of price, subway access, and proximity to most itinerary stops. The Financial District is cheaper and quieter but adds 20–30 minutes to most Day 1 and Day 2 stops. Avoid hotels directly in Times Square — overpriced and noisy with no location advantage. Book 3–4 weeks ahead on weekdays; longer for weekends.

Solo Budget & Logistics.question: Is it worth buying a 7-day unlimited MetroCard for a 3-day NYC visit?
Solo Budget & Logistics.answer: No. A 7-day unlimited MetroCard costs $34 and only makes sense if you take more than 11 subway rides — unlikely on a 3-day trip. Pay-per-ride via OMNY tap (any contactless card or phone) is more flexible and cheaper for most solo itineraries. The exception: if you're doing multiple cross-borough trips each day, the unlimited card pays off by Day 2.
